text: on the wall of the intestine. Empty calories : Term used for foods that provide only energy without any other nutrient, eg. white sugar and alcohol. Enzymes : Biological catalysts which enhance the rate of chemical reactions in the body. Essential fatty acids (EFA) : Fatty acids like linoleic acid and alpha linolenic acid which are not made in the human body and must be supplied through the diet. Fatty acids : Fundamental constitutents of many lipids. Fibre : Collective term for the structural parts of plant tissues which are resistant to the human digestive enzymes. Flavonoids : Pigments widely distributed in nature in flowers, fruits and vegetables. Food Exchange : Foods are classified into different groups for exchange. Each “exchange list” includes a number of measured foods of similar
